mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in real residential construction activity signals sustained household wealth effects and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising housing permit periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% compresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing above 4.5%, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a rolling regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate acceleration signal a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production indicates supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that erode corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not correlate with negative equity returns in a rolling 3-year window, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment expansion reinforces wage growth expectations and aggregate demand, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive short-term equity returns in a walk-forward test, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals weakening consumer confidence and credit availability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million does not precede negative SPX returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the forward return distribution shows no significant left skew.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated inflation and restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary discount rate shock that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to explain variance in 60-day forward returns, or SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when both series are elevated simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth following a recessionary trough signals a cyclical recovery that boosts corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth exceeding 2.5% quarterly does not lead to positive SPX returns in a majority of out-of-sample episodes, or the predictive signal reverses direction during actual recovery phases.
